git_head: da00e9b Record G900 finite boundary type ledger
thesis: A registered trace is boundary-preserving finite aftermath, not measurement, display, transport, channel, or force.
purpose: Define the criterion that lets the physics program move from admissible action to trace without collapsing into viewer or channel language.

criterion_atoms.statement: A registered trace must be sourced in an admitted body.
criterion_atoms.testable_requirement: body artifact exists and is not mutated by the trace record.

criterion_atoms.statement: A trace must arise from an admissible action, relation, support test, or boundary check.
criterion_atoms.testable_requirement: the source audit names the action or relation family being tested.

criterion_atoms.statement: A trace must leave a finite residue, contrast, return, unsupportedness, or correlation profile.
criterion_atoms.testable_requirement: the source audit contains a finite nonempty witness set, count profile, failure profile, or return profile.

criterion_atoms.statement: A trace cannot originate from a viewer, renderer, marker, screenshot, or visual overlay.
criterion_atoms.testable_requirement: the trace source is data/audit/math, not display code or visual inspection.

criterion_atoms.statement: A trace must be reproducible from a named artifact or deterministic construction.
criterion_atoms.testable_requirement: the source artifact has stable JSON, counts, hashes, or explicitly named input files.

criterion_atoms.statement: A trace is not automatically transport, channel, measurement, or physical force.
criterion_atoms.testable_requirement: the trace record must not admit a channel or force claim without a later criterion.

criterion_atoms.statement: Recording the trace must not mutate the body, registry, or boundary grammar.
criterion_atoms.testable_requirement: the trace artifact declares no body mutation, no registry mutation, no renderer authority, no force claim, and no physics claim.

criterion_atoms.statement: A registered trace may later be classified as obstruction, transport frontier, or bridge candidate, but that classification is a separate theorem.
criterion_atoms.testable_requirement: the trace criterion only registers tracehood and does not force downstream classification.

trace_classes.description: A finite leftover pattern after an admissible action or relation.
A finite return, cycle, recurrence, or closed relation profile.
A finite failure, unsupportedness, or boundary-pressure profile.
A finite non-arbitrary correlation profile surviving audit.
A finite change of admissible state, phase, or relation.

admission_rule.statement: An object is a registered trace when all required criterion atoms hold.
